descriptiveIncome.definition: Median after-tax income of economic families and unattached individuals
descriptiveIncome.limitations: This is an overall descriptive reference. It is not matched to one household size, tenure, or geography-specific Market Basket Measure threshold and does not determine the national verdict.

mbm-poverty-rate.label: Share below the Market Basket Measure
mbm-poverty-rate.definition: Percentage of people below Canada's official poverty line under the 2023-base Market Basket Measure
mbm-poverty-rate.limitations: Latest-vintage revised survey estimate; sampling and non-sampling error apply.

deep-poverty-rate.label: Share in deep poverty
deep-poverty-rate.definition: Percentage with income below 75% of Canada's official poverty line
deep-poverty-rate.source: Statistics Canada, Dimensions of Poverty Hub, 2024
deep-poverty-rate.limitations: This is an income-threshold measure, not a direct household budget.

food-insecurity-rate.label: Household food-insecurity exposure
food-insecurity-rate.definition: Percentage of people living in households reporting marginal, moderate, or severe food insecurity
food-insecurity-rate.limitations: The measure captures household food-access experience and is not a food-price index.

shelter-burden-30-rate.label: Households spending at least 30% of income on shelter
shelter-burden-30-rate.definition: Share of households in the provinces spending 30% or more of income on shelter costs
shelter-burden-30-rate.limitations: Older than the income and food-security evidence; provincial household coverage and tenure differences apply.

shelter-burden-50-rate.label: Households spending at least 50% of income on shelter
shelter-burden-50-rate.limitations: The official category is defined, but a verified current national estimate has not yet been integrated.

core-housing-need-rate.label: Households in core housing need
core-housing-need-rate.definition: Share living in unsuitable, inadequate, or unaffordable housing and unable to afford acceptable local alternatives
core-housing-need-rate.limitations: Census-period measure; substantially older than current price and income observations.

representativeBudgets.householdType: Toronto Market Basket Measure reference family of four
representativeBudgets.incomeDefinition: Median after-tax income of Canadian two-parent families with children
representativeBudgets.limitations: Illustrative only. National family income is not matched to the Toronto MBM family by geography and exact composition, so this case cannot determine a national verdict.

matchedHouseholdCases.missing: Matching household-size and geography-specific MBM basket
Matching household-size and geography-specific MBM basket
Matching single-person and geography-specific MBM basket
Matching senior household and geography-specific basic-needs basket

historicalAffordability.geographyLevel: national income deciles matched to each family unit's applicable MBM basket
limitations: Each published decile average represents ten percent of family units, so threshold shares move in ten-percentage-point increments and do not reveal within-decile hardship.
The continuous display uses the 2018-base MBM through 2019 and the 2023-base MBM from 2020; five-year comparisons cross that documented methodology break.
Regional median-family histories are context only and do not determine the national headline.

affordability.unresolvedReason: Matched household-type income and geography-specific basic-needs baskets are incomplete, so the national absolute verdict is unresolved.

us.referencePopulation: People and households in the United States

descriptiveIncome.source: U.S. Census Bureau, Income in the United States: 2024
descriptiveIncome.limitations: Descriptive only; it is not household-type matched and does not determine the national verdict.

official-poverty-rate.label: Official poverty rate
official-poverty-rate.definition: Share below the official U.S. poverty threshold
official-poverty-rate.source: U.S. Census Bureau, Income in the United States: 2024
official-poverty-rate.limitations: Official thresholds vary by family size and composition but not geography.

deep-poverty-rate.label: Share below 50% of the poverty threshold
deep-poverty-rate.source: U.S. Census Bureau, Income in the United States: 2024
deep-poverty-rate.limitations: Income threshold measure, not a direct household budget.

food-insecurity-rate.label: Food-insecure households
food-insecurity-rate.definition: Share of households experiencing food insecurity
food-insecurity-rate.source: USDA Economic Research Service, Household Food Security in the United States in 2024
food-insecurity-rate.limitations: Household experiential measure; not a retail food-price index.

very-low-food-security-rate.label: Households with very low food security
very-low-food-security-rate.definition: Share of households with disrupted eating patterns and reduced food intake
very-low-food-security-rate.limitations: Survey estimate subject to sampling and non-sampling error.

housing-burden-30-rate.label: Households with housing-cost burden
housing-burden-30-rate.definition: Share spending more than 30% of household income on housing costs
housing-burden-30-rate.source: U.S. Census Bureau, ACS Housing Availability and Affordability: 2023
housing-burden-30-rate.limitations: All-household national estimate; renter and owner burdens differ substantially.

housing-burden-50-rate.label: Households with severe housing-cost burden
housing-burden-50-rate.definition: Share spending more than 50% of household income on housing costs
housing-burden-50-rate.source: U.S. Census Bureau, ACS Housing Availability and Affordability: 2023
housing-burden-50-rate.limitations: All-household national estimate; renter and owner burdens differ substantially.

representativeBudgets.source: U.S. Census Bureau and BLS Consumer Expenditure Surveys
representativeBudgets.limitations: Illustrative only. Median Census households and average BLS consumer units are not matched, so this case cannot determine a national verdict.

affordability.unresolvedReason: Matched household-type income and official basic-needs cases are incomplete, so the national absolute verdict is unresolved.

owen-sound.referencePopulation: Households in the Owen Sound city census subdivision (DGUID 2021A00053542059)

descriptiveIncome.definition: Median after-tax income of private households in the Owen Sound city census subdivision
descriptiveIncome.limitations: Structural baseline using 2020 income reported in the 2021 Census; pandemic transfers affected 2020 incomes.

food-insecurity-rate.label: Grey Bruce household food-insecurity prevalence
food-insecurity-rate.definition: Households experiencing food insecurity in the Grey Bruce health-unit geography, 2021 to 2023 combined
food-insecurity-rate.source: Grey Bruce Public Health, Monitoring Food Affordability in Grey Bruce 2024
food-insecurity-rate.limitations: Regional Grey Bruce prevalence, not an Owen Sound city-CSD estimate.

shelter-burden-30-rate.label: Owen Sound households spending at least 30% of income on shelter
shelter-burden-30-rate.definition: Owner and tenant households with positive income spending 30% or more on shelter costs
shelter-burden-30-rate.source: Statistics Canada, 2021 Census Profile, Owen Sound city CSD
shelter-burden-30-rate.limitations: 2021 Census structural baseline using 2020 income; long-form estimates are subject to sampling and random rounding.

owner-shelter-burden-30-rate.label: Owen Sound owner households spending at least 30% on shelter
owner-shelter-burden-30-rate.definition: Owner households spending 30% or more of total income on shelter costs
owner-shelter-burden-30-rate.source: Statistics Canada, 2021 Census Profile, Owen Sound city CSD
owner-shelter-burden-30-rate.limitations: Does not identify current mortgage-renewal exposure.

renter-shelter-burden-30-rate.label: Owen Sound renter households spending at least 30% on shelter
renter-shelter-burden-30-rate.definition: Tenant households spending 30% or more of total income on shelter costs
renter-shelter-burden-30-rate.source: Statistics Canada, 2021 Census Profile, Owen Sound city CSD
renter-shelter-burden-30-rate.limitations: The Census baseline predates recent rental-market changes.

core-housing-need-rate.label: Owen Sound households in core housing need
core-housing-need-rate.definition: Households below an adequacy, suitability, or affordability standard that could not afford acceptable local alternatives
core-housing-need-rate.source: Statistics Canada, 2021 Census Profile, Owen Sound city CSD
core-housing-need-rate.limitations: Excludes households not assessable under the official core-housing-need definition.

owner-core-housing-need-rate.label: Owen Sound owner households in core housing need
owner-core-housing-need-rate.definition: Owner households in core housing need
owner-core-housing-need-rate.source: Statistics Canada, 2021 Census Profile, Owen Sound city CSD

renter-core-housing-need-rate.label: Owen Sound renter households in core housing need
renter-core-housing-need-rate.definition: Tenant households in core housing need
renter-core-housing-need-rate.source: Statistics Canada, 2021 Census Profile, Owen Sound city CSD

contextEvidence.label: Grey Bruce nutritious food basket, family of four
contextEvidence.definition: Monthly cost of the Ontario Nutritious Food Basket for a reference family of four
contextEvidence.source: Grey Bruce Public Health, Monitoring Food Affordability in Grey Bruce 2024
contextEvidence.reason: The measured family-of-four basket was about 1% more expensive than in 2023, adding current regional food-cost pressure.
contextEvidence.limitations: Grey Bruce regional basket, not an Owen Sound-only grocery survey; excludes restaurant food, special diets, and transportation.

contextEvidence.label: Owen Sound-area one-bedroom rental reference
contextEvidence.definition: One-bedroom rent used in Grey Bruce Public Health affordability scenarios from CMHC rental-market data
contextEvidence.source: Grey Bruce Public Health citing CMHC Rental Market Survey
contextEvidence.reason: It provides newer rental-cost context, but it cannot establish a direction against the Census baseline because coverage and building samples differ.
contextEvidence.limitations: Purpose-built buildings with at least three units; utilities may or may not be included and new-tenant rents may be higher.

contextEvidence.label: Owen Sound-area three-bedroom rental reference
contextEvidence.definition: Three-bedroom rent used in Grey Bruce Public Health family affordability scenarios from CMHC rental-market data
contextEvidence.source: Grey Bruce Public Health citing CMHC Rental Market Survey
contextEvidence.reason: It gives a newer family-rental reference while remaining non-comparable with the older city-wide Census shelter-cost measure.
contextEvidence.limitations: Small local rental samples can be suppressed or imprecise; new-tenant rents may be higher.

matchedHouseholdCases.source: Statistics Canada 2021 Census Profile and Tables 11-10-0104-01 / 11-10-0066-01
matchedHouseholdCases.limitations: Median city-CSD income; MBM Ontario 30,000-to-99,999 threshold based on Owen Sound's 32,712-person census agglomeration. Housing scenarios use city-wide tenure medians rather than household-specific costs.

matchedHouseholdCases.source: Statistics Canada 2021 Census Profile and Tables 11-10-0104-01 / 11-10-0066-01
matchedHouseholdCases.limitations: Economic-family income and household shelter-cost concepts are not perfectly identical populations.

matchedHouseholdCases.source: Statistics Canada 2021 Census Profile and Tables 11-10-0104-01 / 11-10-0066-01
matchedHouseholdCases.limitations: Average family size was 3.9 and is matched to the four-person MBM threshold. City-wide tenure costs do not capture bedroom needs.

matchedHouseholdCases.source: Statistics Canada 2021 Census Profile and Tables 11-10-0104-01 / 11-10-0066-01
matchedHouseholdCases.limitations: Average family size was 2.6 and is matched to the three-person MBM threshold. Family composition and actual tenure vary.

historicalAffordability.geographyLevel: annual census-agglomeration context; city-CSD hardship evidence remains authoritative
limitations: Annual tax-file income describes the wider Owen Sound census agglomeration, not the city census subdivision.
City-CSD household distributions are available only for Census years, so annual regional median-family trends cannot determine local direction.
Improving regional medians cannot override city-CSD shelter burden, renter burden, core housing need, food insecurity, or matched city household cases.

affordability.aggregateLimitations: The four Census household categories cover most, but not all, city households and economic-family counts are not a perfect mutually exclusive household distribution. Medians hide within-type hardship.

food.caseStatusBasis: the matched 2020 MBM total threshold; food costs are published separately

housing.caseStatusBasis: the city-CSD renter shelter-cost reference; owner burdens are published separately

evidence.label: Local housing evidence lacks a consistent post-Census time series

global.unsupportedReason: No valid globally comparable absolute household-income and essential-cost distribution is available in the project.
